assignments.value: New File Added to Folder1 Folder

emailSubject
assignments.value: New File Added to Folder2 Folder

assignments.name: emailBody

parameters.content:
# Google Drive Receipt Monitor Workflows

Two n8n workflows that automatically monitor Google Drive folders and send email notifications via Outlook when new receipt files are added.

## Workflows

### 1. google-drive-folder1-monitor.json
Monitors the first Google Drive folder (placeholder: "Folder1")

### 2. google-drive-folder2-monitor.json
Monitors the second Google Drive folder (placeholder: "Folder2")

## Architecture

Each workflow follows the same pattern:
```
Google Drive Trigger вҶ’ Download File вҶ’ Prepare Email Data вҶ’ Send Email via Outlook
```

- **Google Drive Trigger**: Polls folder every minute for new files
- **Download File**: Downloads the file as binary data
- **Prepare Email Data**: Formats email content with folder identification
- **Send Email via Outlook**: Sends notification with file attached

## Prerequisites

Before deploying, you need to set up OAuth2 credentials in your n8n instance.

### 1. Google OAuth2 Credentials

**In Google Cloud Console**:
1. Go to https://console.cloud.google.com
2. Create a new project or select existing
3. Enable Google Drive API
4. Go to "Credentials" вҶ’ "Create Credentials" вҶ’ "OAuth 2.0 Client ID"
5. Application type: Web application
6. Add authorized redirect URI: `https://n8n.204it.ca/rest/oauth2-credential/callback`
7. Copy Client ID and Client Secret

**In n8n**:
1. Go to Settings вҶ’ Credentials
2. Click "Create New Credential"
3. Select "Google OAuth2 API"
4. Paste Client ID and Client Secret
5. Add scope: `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive.readonly`
6. Click "Connect to Google" and authorize
7. Save the credential

### 2. Microsoft Outlook OAuth2 Credentials

**In Azure Portal**:
1. Go to https://portal.azure.com
2. Navigate to Azure Active Directory вҶ’ App registrations
3. Click "New registration"
4. Name: "n8n Outlook Integration"
5. Add redirect URI: `https://n8n.204it.ca/rest/oauth2-credential/callback`
6. Go to "Certificates & secrets" вҶ’ Create new client secret
7. Copy the secret value (shown only once)
8. Go to "API permissions" вҶ’ Add permission вҶ’ Microsoft Graph вҶ’ Delegated permissions
9. Add: `Mail.Send`
10. Copy Application (client) ID and Directory (tenant) ID from Overview page

**In n8n**:
1. Go to Settings вҶ’ Credentials
2. Click "Create New Credential"
3. Select "Microsoft Outlook OAuth2 API"
4. Paste Application ID, Directory ID, and Client Secret
5. Click "Connect to Microsoft" and authorize
6. Save the credential

## Deployment Steps

### Option A: Import via n8n UI (Recommended)

1. Open your n8n instance: https://n8n.204it.ca
2. Click "Add workflow" вҶ’ "Import from File"
3. Select [google-drive-folder1-monitor.json](google-drive-folder1-monitor.json)
4. The workflow will open in the editor
5. Configure credentials:
   - Click on "Google Drive Trigger - Folder1" node
   - Select your Google OAuth2 credential
   - Click on "Download File" node
   - Select the same Google OAuth2 credential
   - Click on "Send Email via Outlook" node
   - Select your Microsoft Outlook OAuth2 credential
6. Update placeholder values (see Configuration section below)
7. Click "Save"
8. Repeat steps 2-7 for [google-drive-folder2-monitor.json](google-drive-folder2-monitor.json)

### Option B: Deploy via n8n API

```bash
# Deploy Workflow 1
curl -X POST https://n8n.204it.ca/api/v1/workflows \
  -H "X-N8N-API-KEY: your-api-key-here" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d @google-drive-folder1-monitor.json

# Deploy Workflow 2
curl -X POST https://n8n.204it.ca/api/v1/workflows \
  -H "X-N8N-API-KEY: your-api-key-here" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d @google-drive-folder2-monitor.json
```

Note: API key can be found in your `.mcp.json` file (but don't commit it to git!)

## Configuration

After importing, you MUST update these placeholder values in each workflow:

### Workflow 1: google-drive-folder1-monitor

**Node: "Google Drive Trigger - Folder1"**
- Current value: `"Folder1"`
- Change to: Your actual first folder name (case-sensitive)

**Node: "Prepare Email Data"** - Update all 3 values:
1. `folderName`: Change from `"Folder1"` to actual folder name
2. `recipientEmail`: Change from `"recipient@example.com"` to actual recipient email
3. `emailSubject`: Update "Folder1" reference to match actual folder name
4. `emailBody`: Update "Folder1" reference to match actual folder name

### Workflow 2: google-drive-folder2-monitor

**Node: "Google Drive Trigger - Folder2"**
- Current value: `"Folder2"`
- Change to: Your actual second folder name (case-sensitive)

**Node: "Prepare Email Data"** - Update all 3 values:
1. `folderName`: Change from `"Folder2"` to actual folder name
2. `recipientEmail`: Change from `"recipient@example.com"` to actual recipient email
3. `emailSubject`: Update "Folder2" reference to match actual folder name
4. `emailBody`: Update "Folder2" reference to match actual folder name

### Quick Configuration Checklist

- [ ] Import both workflows to n8n
- [ ] Connect Google OAuth2 credentials to all Google Drive nodes
- [ ] Connect Microsoft Outlook OAuth2 credentials to Outlook nodes
- [ ] Update Folder1 name in workflow 1
- [ ] Update Folder2 name in workflow 2
- [ ] Update recipient email in both workflows
- [ ] Update email subject/body to match actual folder names
- [ ] Test both workflows manually
- [ ] Activate both workflows

## Testing

### Manual Test (Recommended Before Activation)

1. Open workflow in n8n editor
2. Click "Execute Workflow" button (play icon)
3. Manually add a test file to the monitored Google Drive folder
4. Wait up to 1 minute for polling
5. Check execution in n8n:
   - Should show green success
   - Check each node output
   - Verify binary data in "Download File" node
6. Check recipient email inbox:
   - Should receive email within 1-2 minutes
   - Verify correct subject line
   - Verify correct folder name in body
   - Verify file is attached and opens correctly

### Live Test

1. Activate workflow by toggling "Active" switch
2. Upload real receipt file to folder
3. Wait for notification email (max 1 minute)
4. Verify email content and attachment
5. Repeat for second folder

### Troubleshooting Failed Tests

**No execution triggered**:
- Check workflow is activated (green toggle)
- Verify folder name is exact match (case-sensitive)
- Check Google OAuth2 credential is valid
- Wait full minute (polling interval)

**Execution fails at Download node**:
- Verify file still exists in Google Drive
- Check OAuth2 permissions include drive.readonly
- Verify file is not corrupted

**Execution fails at Email node**:
- Check Outlook OAuth2 credential is valid
- Verify Mail.Send permission granted
- Check recipient email is valid
- Check attachment size < 50MB

## Email Template

When a file is added, the recipient receives:

**Subject**: `New File Added to [FolderName] Folder`

**Body**:
```
A new file has been added to your Google Drive folder.

Folder: [FolderName]
File Name: [FileName]
Added: [Timestamp]

The file is attached to this email.
```

**Attachment**: Original file from Google Drive

## How It Works

### Polling Mechanism

- Workflows check folders **every minute**
- Google Drive Trigger node maintains state to detect new files
- Only fires when new files are created (not updates/deletes)
- Processes one file per execution

### Binary Data Handling

1. **Download**: File stored as binary data in `$binary.data`
2. **Pass-through**: Binary data automatically flows through Set node
3. **Attach**: Outlook node reads from `binaryPropertyName: "data"`
4. No manual encoding needed - n8n handles it

### File Size Limits

- n8n default max: **50MB** per file
- Files larger than limit will fail at Download or Email node
- To handle larger files, consider sending Google Drive share link instead of attachment

## Monitoring and Maintenance

### Check Execution History

1. Open workflow in n8n
2. Click "Executions" tab
3. Review recent executions:
   - Green = Success
   - Red = Failed
4. Click execution to see details and node outputs

### Common Maintenance Tasks

**Weekly**:
- Review execution history for errors
- Check email delivery (ask recipient)

**Monthly**:
- Check API quota usage in Google Cloud Console
- Verify OAuth2 credentials still valid

**As Needed**:
- Update folder names if folders renamed in Google Drive
- Update recipient email address
- Adjust polling interval (if needed)

### Changing Folder Names

If you rename folders in Google Drive:
1. Open workflow in n8n
2. Edit "Google Drive Trigger" node
3. Update "Folder to Watch" value
4. Edit "Prepare Email Data" node
5. Update `folderName`, `emailSubject`, and `emailBody` to match
6. Save workflow

### Changing Recipient Email

1. Open workflow in n8n
2. Edit "Prepare Email Data" node
3. Update `recipientEmail` value
4. Save workflow

### Adjusting Polling Interval

Current: Every minute (fastest, most API usage)

To change:
1. Open workflow
2. Edit "Google Drive Trigger" node
3. Expand "Options"
4. Change "Poll Times" to:
   - Every minute
   - Every 5 minutes
   - Every 15 minutes
   - Every hour
   - Custom interval
5. Save workflow

## API Quota Usage

### Google Drive API
- **Free tier**: 20,000 requests/day per user
- **Our usage**: 2 workflows Г— 1,440 polls/day = 2,880 requests/day
- **Percentage**: ~14% of free tier
- **Cost**: $0/month

### Microsoft Graph API (Outlook)
- **Free tier**: 10,000 requests/day per app
- **Our usage**: Variable (depends on file additions)
- Typical: <100 requests/day
- **Cost**: $0/month

Both APIs are well within free tiers.

## Security Considerations

- OAuth2 tokens stored encrypted in n8n database
- Credentials never exposed in workflow JSON
- `.mcp.json` file contains API key - **do not commit to git**
- `.gitignore` configured to protect sensitive files
- Email recipient hardcoded (not user-controlled)
- Consider using Google service account for production

## Workflow Structure Details

### Node Flow

```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ Google Drive Trigger        в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ - Monitors specific folder  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ - Polls every minute        в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ - Detects new files only    в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
           в”Ӯ
           в–ј
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ Download File               в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ - Gets file by ID           в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ - Stores as binary data     в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ - Property: "data"          в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
           в”Ӯ
           в–ј
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ Prepare Email Data          в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ - Sets folder name          в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ - Sets recipient email      в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ - Formats subject/body      в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
           в”Ӯ
           в–ј
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ Send Email via Outlook      в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ - Sends to recipient        в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ - Attaches binary file      в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ - Uses email template       в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

### Expression Syntax Used

- `={{ $json.id }}` - File ID from trigger
- `={{ $json.name }}` - File name from trigger
- `={{ $json.createdTime }}` - Timestamp from trigger
- `={{ $json.recipientEmail }}` - From Set node
- `={{ $json.emailSubject }}` - From Set node
- `={{ $json.emailBody }}` - From Set node

## Extending the Workflows

### Add More Recipients

In "Prepare Email Data" node, change:
```json
"recipientEmail": "email1@example.com,email2@example.com,email3@example.com"
```

### Add More Folders

Simply clone an existing workflow and update folder references.

### Add Slack Notifications

After "Prepare Email Data" node:
1. Add Slack node
2. Configure channel and message
3. Connect in parallel with Outlook node

### Add Error Notifications

1. Add "Error Trigger" node
2. Connect to Slack/Email node
3. Send alert when workflow fails

### Filter by File Type

After "Google Drive Trigger", add IF node:
```javascript
// Only process PDF files
{{ $json.mimeType === 'application/pdf' }}
```

## Support and Resources

- **n8n Instance**: https://n8n.204it.ca
- **n8n Documentation**: https://docs.n8n.io
- **Google Drive API Docs**: https://developers.google.com/drive
- **Microsoft Graph API Docs**: https://docs.microsoft.com/graph
- **Plan file**: `/Users/ridalakhani/.claude/plans/atomic-hopping-creek.md`

## Changelog

### 2026-01-28 - Initial Creation
- Created two workflow files for monitoring Google Drive folders
- Configured every-minute polling for responsive notifications
- Set up Outlook email integration with attachments
- Added comprehensive documentation
